_comment: Reference for .mcp.json — the agent's entire tool surface. Copy the servers you want into .mcp.json. Shown: an MCP gateway/aggregator over HTTP (the ideal — front many upstream servers behind one endpoint), plus individual servers listed directly (Linear's official hosted server, a stdio server, an authenticated HTTP server). Anything you add becomes mcp__<name>__<tool> tools the agent can call. Use ${ENV_VAR} so secrets stay in the environment, not in this file. This layer is independent of the daemon's LINEAR_API_TOKEN, which only drives the poll/post harness.

gateway._comment: An MCP gateway/aggregator over HTTP — the ideal: front many upstream MCP servers behind one endpoint, then add or remove upstreams there with no change here. Swap this back into .mcp.json (replacing the linear entry, or alongside it) to route the agent through your gateway. ${MCP_AGGREGATOR_URL} is read from .env and defaults to localhost:3000.

linear._comment: Linear's official hosted MCP server — gives the agent Linear tools (search/create/update issues, comments, projects). Authenticate it with any Linear credential; reusing the daemon's LINEAR_API_TOKEN is convenient. This is the agent's tool layer, separate from the poll/post harness that also uses that token.
